title: The Clock Tower Becomes the Key
description: On pages 14–17, this part focuses on the clock tower becomes the key, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Clock Tower Becomes the Key. The bomb’s location and countdown push the Alubarna battle into its final desperate stage. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I wonder... if the others are alright...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: How can you still stand... Did you somehow dodge all the falling rubble even after getting so thoroughly cut up!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: ...Dodge? All these rocks...? No...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I knew... I knew which place the rocks wouldn't fall...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: My sword...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Anyway...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: It's under-neath the rock over there::

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I know it...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What're you-

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I've had this feeling before... when all around me becomes quiet...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: And my own heartbeat beats loudly as a drum... this is the boundary between life and death!...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: These falling rocks...I can sense a kind of presence about them, as if they were living things...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: No, nota presence... something more definite... almost like a...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: "Breath"...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: The breath... book..

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: As does the soil...

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: ...could this be it?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Listen well, zero. There are swordsmen in this world who can't cut anything.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: to be able to cut nothing... Does that mean to know the "breath" of things...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Is that... the ability to cut through iron!?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: What the hell did you do!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: How is it possible that you could still be standing after taking so much damage and losing that much blood!? It's not possible!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I can hear it... the breath of his iron too...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: ...I don't know what trick you pulled, but I'll make sure to kill you for good this time.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Convey my will to the blade...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: SPLIT...

summary: This part develops the clock tower becomes the key and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
